content: Hey, I'm so frustrated with my current laptop. It's slowing me down and I need something that can handle all my real estate software.
Sorry to hear that, Ellis. What kind of software do you use most often for your real estate work?
I use a lot of CRM tools like Salesforce, and I also do some video editing for my social media channels. I've been thinking of getting a MacBook Pro, but I'm not sure if it's worth the investment.
MacBook Pros are great for video editing. Have you considered any other options like the Dell XPS or HP Envy?

Yeah, I've looked at those, but I've heard great things about Macs for creative work. I've also been wanting to get into painting and urban sketching, so I think the MacBook's display would be perfect for that.
That's a great point. The MacBook's display is top-notch. What kind of painting and sketching do you like to do? Are you self-taught or have you taken classes?
I'm self-taught, but I've been taking some online classes to learn more about watercolor and ink. I love sketching the cityscapes of Austin, where I used to live.
Austin is a great city for inspiration. Do you find that your real estate work influences your art at all?
Actually, yes. I've been able to use my knowledge of the city to find great locations to sketch. And, I've even sold a few pieces to clients who appreciate the local scenery.
That's wonderful. It's great that you've been able to combine your passions. Now, back to your laptop search... what's your budget looking like?
I'm thinking of spending around $2,000. I've also been considering getting a tablet to use with my laptop, maybe an iPad Pro.
The iPad Pro is a great choice. It's perfect for sketching and note-taking. Have you thought about how you'll use it in conjunction with your laptop?
Yeah, I think I'll use it for meetings and presentations. I've got a big meeting coming up on March 15th, and I want to make sure I'm prepared.

March 15th is just around the corner. I'm sure you'll do great. Just to confirm, you're looking for a laptop and tablet combo that can handle your real estate software, video editing, and artistic pursuits, all within a budget of $2,000?
That's right. And, I've got to make sure it's all set up by the time my parents come visit me in Denver on April 1st. I want to be able to show them all the new tech I've got.
I've got it all noted down, Ellis. I'll make sure to recommend the best options for you. And, I hope you have a great time with your parents in Denver!
Thanks, I'm looking forward to it. I've been taking care of them a lot lately, so it'll be nice to have some quality time together.
